name: Developer-local hybrid reality
current_usage_summary: Practitioners rarely pick MCP or CLI exclusively. Typical repo: MCP for SaaS connectors (Linear, Sentry, browser automation) + shell for git/npm/tests + optional HTTP tools. Unified clients emerge (agents-cli.sh) syncing MCP config, skills, hooks across Claude/Cursor/Codex. AGENTS.md and .cursor/rules document when to use which. Sandboxed cloud agents skew MCP; local agents skew shell. 'Hybrid' is operational default per Anthropic's mature-integration framing: API foundation, CLI local, MCP cloud.
typical_stack: Project: .cursor/mcp.json + AGENTS.md + skills. Global: ~/.cursor/mcp.json, ~/.claude/settings. Browser MCP for verification. Firecrawl/playwright MCP vs curl. gh in terminal for PRs while GitHub MCP disabled to save tokens.
auth_and_identity_model: Split brain: OAuth in MCP clients for SaaS; OS credentials for CLIs. Same GitHub identity may exist in both gh and GitHub MCP with no linkage. Developers consciously disable heavy MCP servers locally.
context_and_token_behavior: Manual optimization: turn off MCP servers not needed for task; use CLI for GitHub; enable tool search in Claude Code when available. Skills reduce trial-and-error without schema cost. Context budgeting is artisanal—not platform-enforced.
operational_pain_points: Two auth stories to refresh. Rules drift from actual config. Agents pick wrong surface (MCP vs gh) unless rules explicit. Onboarding new teammate requires explaining hybrid policy.
developer_experience_friction: Cognitive load: which integration for which task. Duplicate capability (gh vs github MCP). Testing agent behavior requires both surfaces configured.
gax_mapping: GAX explicitly targets unifying hybrid into one runtime: SaaS and CLI-backed commands both register in gaxd with same envelope and caps. Invocation plane stays CLI-mental (`gax …`); control plane centralizes OAuth + vault; lazy discovery replaces 'disable MCP server' hacks.
migration_phases: 1) Document current hybrid map in repo. 2) Register all SaaS+CLI paths as GAX commands. 3) Collapse mcp.json to single gax-bridge server. 4) Simplify AGENTS.md to GAX-only external actions. 5) Remove duplicate MCP entries.
adapter_or_bridge_strategy: Parallel run: keep MCP for UI-only tools; route data-plane ops through GAX. agents-cli style sync can distribute gax manifests + cap policies across agents.
breaking_changes_and_mitigations: Team habits ('just use gh')—mitigate with linter on AGENTS.md in PR review. Temporary dual config documented in migration runbook.
infrastructure_changes: One gaxd per dev machine replaces N MCP stdio processes for wrapped services—potential RAM win. Org publishes manifest bundle git submodule.
deployment_topology: Local gaxd + optional team registry URL for manifest updates. Cloud agents use hosted gaxd with same command names—dev/prod parity.
adoption_drivers: Eliminates hybrid ops complexity—one grammar, one audit stream. Matches how developers already want to work (short commands, turn off bloat).
adoption_blockers: Works until one tool lacks GAX manifest—forces fallback hybrid again. Sunk cost in tuned rules per repo.
adoption_timeline_estimate: Early adopters in this repo prototype: immediate; mainstream hybrid teams: 18–36 months as manifest coverage grows.
historical_parallel: Consolidating npm scripts + Makefile + raw binaries into task runner (just, mise)—one interface, many backends.
